title: rosa viscaria subsp. micrantha
intro: Rosa viscaria subsp. micrantha, also known as the alpine rose or dwarf alpine rose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the rose family (Rosaceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help to soothe skin irritations and protect against cell damage. The health benefits of Rosa viscaria subsp. micrantha include its ability to improve digestion, reduce fever, and act as an antiseptic, making it a valuable remedy in traditional medicine. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and salves, which can be applied topically to the skin or consumed orally to reap its medicinal benefits.
